beforeStartingDescription:
Building your custom model is a crucial step to maximize your revenue opportunities.
We strongly recommend that you first carefully review the "STRATEGIC TIPS AND GUIDELINES" section.
These recommendations will help you design a model fully adapted to your hotel's category, capacity, and audience profile.
Once you have studied the guidelines, you can proceed to download and use the Online Calculator located to the right.

buildYourModel.title: BUILD YOUR OWN MODEL
buildYourModel.description: Create a custom pricing model for your hotel

utilitiesGuestsNotTransients: HOTEL-LIVING GUESTS ARE NOT TRANSIENT TOURISTS
utilitiesDescription1: They are respectful long-term residents who stay for 8, 16, 24, or 32 days.
utilitiesDescription2: Due to the longer stay duration and the more personal relationship they establish with your property, these guests tend to be more mindful in their resource usage.
utilitiesDescription3: They feel more like temporary tenants than short-stay visitors — and that makes all the difference.
utilitiesLowerConsumption: UTILITY CONSUMPTION IS LOWER AND MORE STABLE
utilitiesEnergyEfficiency: ENERGY EFFICIENCY SYSTEMS (like LED lighting and moderate climate control) are even more effective
utilitiesConservativeHabits: FEELING AT HOME, guests naturally adopt more conservative habits
utilitiesResponsibilityDescription:
Since Hotel-Living offers accessible rates, guests are generally mindful and respectful of the opportunity they're given.
They know this model depends on balance, care, and sustainability — and most act accordingly.
utilitiesOptimizedDescription: Thanks to lower room turnover and simplified cleaning protocols, utility usage remains constant and optimized, with fewer peaks in electricity or water demand.
cleaningHotelLivingStandard: HOTEL-LIVING MEANS A NEW STANDARD OF HOSPITALITY
cleaningFeelsLikeHome: ONE THAT FEELS MORE LIKE HOME.
cleaningDescription1: Unlike traditional hotels with daily cleaning, our approach reflects the pace of real life.
cleaningDescription2: Just like someone living at home might receive light help a few times a week, our guests enjoy attentive and discreet care that respects their independence and comfort.
cleaningCompleteDescription: A thorough room cleaning every 7 days ensures hygiene and freshness, with minimal intrusion.
cleaningLightRefreshDescription: Optional small touch-ups — 🗑️ trash removal, 🧺 light tidying, 🌬️ ventilation — are available, respecting privacy and lifestyle.
cleaningBedChangeDescription: Sheets and towels are renewed regularly, following home comfort standards.
cleaningOperationalDescription:
This model reduces operational costs for hotels, while offering guests a residential experience:

•	Fewer interruptions
•	More privacy
•	The feeling of truly belonging — not just staying.

section1.title: DOES YOUR HOTEL HAVE TO CLOSE FOR LONG PERIODS EACH YEAR?
section1.content: Most hotels have certain periods during the year when demand drops significantly, leading to partial or full closures. By adopting a flexible living model, you will keep rooms filled during these low seasons, stabilizing income and improving operational efficiency year-round.

section2.title: BUILD A MODEL THAT GUARANTEES FULL OCCUPANCY
section2.content: The Hotel Living model is designed to secure 100% occupancy year-round and maximize revenue through volume. By offering long-stay packages at strategically optimized rates—low enough to keep every room filled but high enough to ensure substantial earnings—hotels can transform vacant nights into predictable, continuous income. This approach shifts the focus from high nightly rates to large cumulative gains driven by extended stays.

section3.title: USE AFFINITIES TO DIFFERENTIATE AND TARGET AUDIENCES
section3.content: Focus on niche audiences (artists, digital nomads, retirees, etc.) to avoid competing directly with standard hotel offerings and OTAs. Use affinities to build loyal, recurring client groups adapted to your category (see "Themes" section).

section4.title: ADAPT LENGTH OF STAY TO HOTEL SIZE AND DYNAMICS
section4.content: Smaller hotels may benefit from longer minimum stays (e.g., 1–3 months), reducing turnover cost. Larger hotels can experiment with rotations—e.g., "9 nights per month club"—to optimize for higher volume and guest experience.

section5.title: LEVERAGE BOUTIQUE CONCEPTS FOR SMALL HIGH-END HOTELS
section5.content: If you manage a boutique or luxury hotel, design tailored experiences (wellness, culture, business retreats) with premium rates. These can be highly attractive to remote professionals or guests seeking exclusivity and added value.

section6.title: APPLY VOLUME LOGIC FOR LARGER HOTELS
section6.content: Larger properties can tap into "living at scale" logic—discounts for recurring guests, flexible plans (e.g., alternate weeks), and partnerships with institutions or companies to fill blocks of rooms efficiently.

section7.title: UNDERSTAND THE POWER OF RECURRING STAYS AND CUSTOMER ROTATION
section7.content: Monthly, multi-month, or club-style stays (where guests return on set days each month) increase forecastability while giving guests "home" status. This increases loyalty and can transform your hotel's business model in a predictable, positive way.

section8.title: HOTEL LIVING PRICING POLICY
section8.content:
HOTEL LIVING PRICING POLICY

The Hotel-Living.com revolution has arrived to transform society, multiply the economic benefits of hotels, and update the outdated lifestyles of a large part of the population, which in many cases generates unwanted household chores, isolation, loneliness, insecurity, among other problems.

It is essential to understand that maximum benefit is obtained by ensuring 100% occupancy every night of the year, not by making small discounts that only manage to rent a few more rooms.

🌐 Two mindsets to maximize hotel success
In hotel management, it is essential to understand that there are two complementary mindsets:

a) The usual mindset:
Trying to rent as many rooms as possible at the highest possible price.

b) The Hotel-Living.com mindset:
Applied to rooms that traditionally remain empty, where the key is not the high price, but avoiding emptiness.

Our model is based on:

Long stays of 8, 15, 22 or 29 days.

Fixed check-in and check-out dates, chosen by the hotel itself, which eliminate nights without occupancy, among many other advantages.

Optimization of staff and resources, thanks to a predictable calendar.

It is essential to understand that the real benefit for each hotel does not come from renting a few more rooms, but from achieving a full hotel every night of the year.
That is the goal of the Hotel-Living.com revolution: full hotels and satisfied customers.

Pricing rules

General rule
The maximum rate that can be charged through Hotel Living for stays of 8, 15, 22 or 29 days will always be, at most, 60% of the hotel's official standard rate per night.

Services included
That rate must include exactly the same services as the hotel's official rate. That is:

If the official rate includes accommodation only → the price on Hotel Living will be up to 60% of that rate, with accommodation only.

If the official rate includes breakfast → the price on Hotel Living will be up to 60% of that rate, with breakfast included.

If the official rate includes half board or full board → the price on Hotel Living will be up to 60% of that rate, with half board or full board included.

Additional rule for hotels with "Accommodation only" or "Accommodation with breakfast" rates
In case a hotel whose base rate does not include half board or full board wishes to offer it to Hotel Living guests, they may do so, but in no case may the total price exceed 100% of their usual official rate and, of course, for the benefit of the hotel itself, it is recommended that this rate for 8, 15, 22 or 29 nights, identical to the traditional customer for just one night, include full board.

This means that a guest staying 29 days should never pay the same as someone staying just one night, unless they enjoy full board included.

Additional discount rule
This 60% percentage is the maximum. Using our Hotel-Living Calculator (available in this same hotel panel) you can verify the great accumulated benefits that can be obtained through greater discounts.

Verification rule
Hotel Living will verify the official rates of each property on other booking platforms.
Any discrepancy or attempt to inflate the base rate will automatically disqualify the hotel from participating in Hotel Living.
